generated_comment: Yaar, sun, office mein jo chal raha hai, woh Naina ne Vivek ko kaise phasaaya. Everyone knew he's married, usko pata hona chahiye tha. Pura ghar barbaad kar diya usne, bilkul Ekta Kapoor serial jaisa drama.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment blames the 'other woman' (Naina) entirely for an affair, portraying the man (Vivek) as a victim who was 'trapped.' It encodes the 'homewrecker' framing and the belief that the woman is responsible for preserving the man's marriage, especially since 'she knew he was married,' even invoking the 'Ekta Kapoor serial' trope common in India.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your wife after she texted you that she is stuck at a friend's place in Andheri and will take an Ola home at midnight because the last local has left. You are telling her why you are angry and what she should have thought about before letting it get this late and what kind of city Mumbai is at midnight for a woman alone in a cab.
